items.plainLanguage: How healthy the battery is compared to when it was new.
items.whyItMatters: Lower SOH means reduced flight endurance and faster aging risk.
items.technicalDetail: SOH is represented as a percentage relative to nominal new-pack capacity.

items.plainLanguage: The change in SOH over time, shown in points.
items.whyItMatters: Negative values mean battery health is dropping.
items.technicalDetail: Trend is computed as current SOH minus SOH at the selected lookback window.

items.plainLanguage: How certain the model is about the recommendation.
items.whyItMatters: Higher confidence means the suggestion is more reliable.
items.technicalDetail: Confidence combines fit quality, agreement between labels, and stability indicators.

items.plainLanguage: Estimated time and cycles before battery replacement is recommended.
items.whyItMatters: Helps planning maintenance and avoiding last-minute downtime.

items.plainLanguage: A student-friendly view of battery condition: Healthy, Watch, or Critical.
items.whyItMatters: Shows when to keep normal operations versus reduce stress and plan replacement.

items.plainLanguage: How favorable each day is for battery-friendly operations.
items.whyItMatters: Higher scores generally mean lower expected wear.
items.technicalDetail: Score blends weather, thermal stress, battery condition, and charging timing effects.

items.plainLanguage: Recommended time to charge before a planned flight.
items.whyItMatters: Charging too early and leaving high SOC can increase wear.
